content.text: Image Generation Tool

Image generation in an agentic workflow means giving a language model the ability to create images on demand — either by calling a dedicated image API directly or by invoking it through a custom tool. Neither Claude nor OpenAI Chat Completions exposes image generation as a single built-in tool the way web search is. Instead, you expose DALL-E, Stable Diffusion, or any other image API as a callable tool and let the LLM decide when and how to use it.

attrs.alt: Two approaches to image generation: OpenAI direct API vs custom tool pattern
attrs.title: Image Generation Approaches

content.text: OpenAI's
client.images.generate()
(gpt-image-1 or DALL-E 3) is a direct API call, not a tool the model invokes. For Claude or multi-model pipelines, expose image generation as a custom tool with a
generate_image
function the model can call.
Generating Images Directly with OpenAI
For workflows where image generation is a final step rather than an agentic decision, call the images API directly. OpenAI's
gpt-image-1
model returns the image as a base64-encoded PNG.

from openai import OpenAI
import base64, pathlib

client = OpenAI()

response = client.images.generate(
    model="gpt-image-1",
    prompt="A serene mountain lake at sunset with pine trees reflected in the water, photorealistic",
    n=1,
    size="1024x1024",
    response_format="b64_json"
)

# Decode and save the image
image_data = response.data[0].b64_json
img_bytes = base64.b64decode(image_data)
pathlib.Path("output.png").write_bytes(img_bytes)
print(f"Image saved: {len(img_bytes):,} bytes")
Exposing Image Generation as a Claude Tool
To let Claude decide when to generate an image, define a
generate_image
tool with a prompt parameter. Claude calls the tool with a refined prompt; your handler calls the image API and returns a URL or confirmation string.
Image Editing and Variations
OpenAI also supports image editing (inpainting) and variations. Expose these as separate tool definitions to give Claude fine-grained control over the image workflow.
Stable Diffusion via Replicate
For open-model image generation, Replicate hosts Stable Diffusion and other models with a simple HTTP API. Wrap it as a custom tool to use with any LLM.
Returning Images to the User

A generated image can be returned to the model as a vision input for verification or captioning — useful in pipelines where Claude should describe or quality-check what was produced before presenting it to the user.

import anthropic, base64, pathlib
from openai import OpenAI

claude = anthropic.Anthropic()
oai    = OpenAI()

def generate_and_verify(description: str) -> str:
    """Generate an image and ask Claude to describe it for quality verification."""
    # Generate the image
    resp = oai.images.generate(
        model="dall-e-3",
        prompt=description,
        n=1,
        size="1024x1024",
        response_format="b64_json"
    )
    b64 = resp.data[0].b64_json

    # Feed the generated image back to Claude for verification
    verify = claude.messages.create(
        model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
        max_tokens=512,
        messages=[{
            "role": "user",
            "content": [
                {
                    "type": "image",
                    "source": {
                        "type": "base64",
                        "media_type": "image/png",
                        "data": b64
                    }
                },
                {
                    "type": "text",
                    "text": f"The image was generated from: '{description}'. "
                           "Does it accurately depict the description? Describe what you see in one sentence."
                }
            ]
        }]
    )
    caption = verify.content[0].text
    # Save the image
    pathlib.Path("verified.png").write_bytes(base64.b64decode(b64))
    return caption

result = generate_and_verify("a red vintage bicycle leaning against a yellow wall")
print(result)
Prompt Refinement Before Generation
LLMs are better at writing detailed image prompts than most users. A two-step pattern — ask the LLM to expand the prompt first, then generate — produces noticeably higher-quality images.

import anthropic
from openai import OpenAI
import base64, pathlib

claude = anthropic.Anthropic()
oai    = OpenAI()

def refine_prompt(user_idea: str) -> str:
    """Expand a vague user idea into a detailed image prompt."""
    resp = claude.messages.create(
        model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
        max_tokens=256,
        messages=[{
            "role": "user",
            "content": (
                f"Expand the following into a detailed, vivid image generation prompt "
                f"(2-4 sentences, mention lighting, style, and mood): '{user_idea}'"
            )
        }]
    )
    return resp.content[0].text.strip()

def generate(prompt: str) -> pathlib.Path:
    resp = oai.images.generate(
        model="dall-e-3",
        prompt=prompt,
        n=1,
        size="1024x1024",
        response_format="b64_json"
    )
    out = pathlib.Path("refined_output.png")
    out.write_bytes(base64.b64decode(resp.data[0].b64_json))
    return out

user_idea = "a cozy coffee shop"
refined   = refine_prompt(user_idea)
print(f"Refined prompt:\n{refined}\n")

path = generate(refined)
print(f"Image saved to: {path}")
Summary
Neither Claude nor OpenAI Chat Completions has a native built-in image generation tool — expose it via
generate_image
custom tool definitions.
OpenAI's
client.images.generate()
(gpt-image-1, DALL-E 3) is a direct API call returning base64 PNG or a URL.
For Claude-based pipelines, define a
generate_image
tool, run the standard tool loop, and call the image API in the handler.
Use the two-step pattern — LLM prompt refinement then generation — for significantly higher-quality results from vague user inputs.
Feed generated images back to Claude as vision input for quality verification or captioning within the same pipeline.
Replicate and similar platforms host Stable Diffusion and other open models behind a simple HTTP API, wrappable as custom tools for any LLM.
